title: Register files and calling conventions
description: Each ABI splits registers into caller-saved (scratch) and callee-saved (preserved) and assigns specific registers to arguments and return values.
body: Use when reasoning about register usage across calls. The ABI designates argument registers (e.g. rdi/rsi/rdx... on System V x86-64, x0-x7 on AArch64), a return register, caller-saved registers a callee may freely trash, and callee-saved registers a callee must preserve. Do not assume a register survives a call unless it is callee-saved, and do not clobber a callee-saved register without restoring it. Actions: consult the ABI table for the target and track which class each register is. Validation signals include correct interop with compiled code across the input range. Risk boundary: confusing caller- and callee-saved roles silently corrupts values across calls; the symptom is remote from the cause, so verify register classes against the ABI rather than guessing.
